logs.name: wary-gazebo
logs.text:
a $200-300 bonus for all employees is better than nothing, but I think there are many companies where he payout would be much greater for the employees. for example, [this article](https://www.cnbc.com/2018/05/09/how-much-the-5-highest-paid-ceos-earn.html) says the CEO of CBS made a salary of $69.3 million and that the CEO of Broadcom made $103.2 million in 2017. according to Forbes, CBS has 16,730 employees, and Broadcom has 14,000 employees (sorry for not having 2017 numbers). cutting the salary of these CEOs to a generous $250,000/year, that’s $69.05 million and $102.95 million extra.

$69,050,000/16,730 employees = $4,127.32/employee

$102,950,000/14,000 employees = $7,353.57/employee

as shown here, there are certainly cases where cutting the CEO’s salary would be very helpful to thousands of people. additionally, I think this should be scaled to help the lower income employees more than those next in line under the CEO. if this kind of redistribution happened nationwide or worldwide, it would mean a lot of money back in the hands of lower and middle class people who work hard everyday to just make ends meet.

logs.name: threaded-skew
logs.text:
Throwing money at people doesn't fix poverty. 

I'm sorry for such a low effort post, but that's the only relevant thing I can think of to respond with. I can somewhat agree that such a large gap between the rich and poor is not a good thing, however the problem is not able to be addressed in a fundamentally effective manner.

logs.name: bronze-circle
logs.text: @threaded-skew Yeah, but it's like saying we shouldn't give water to a thirsty person because it doesn't fix the drought. We gotta start somewhere, right? And if these CEOs can afford to live like kings on a fraction of their current salary, why not? It's not about throwing money, it's about fairness. Plus, imagine what $4,000 could do for a family struggling to pay rent or buy groceries. It's not a solution to poverty, but it's a step towards making things a bit more equal. And @wary-gazebo, you're right, it's crazy how much these CEOs make. It's like they're living in a different world.

logs.name: sour-quasar
logs.text: "I'm all for fairness, but let's not forget that these CEOs are often the ones who have built these companies from the ground up. They've taken risks and made tough decisions. However, I do believe that there should be a limit to how much they earn, especially when their employees are struggling. It's about balance and responsibility. And @threaded-skew, while throwing money at poverty isn't a solution, it's a start. We can't ignore the immediate needs of people. Let's not forget that these bonuses could mean the world to some families. It's not just about the money, it's about giving people a chance to breathe and maybe even dream a little."

logs.name: wary-gazebo
logs.text: "I get what you're saying @sour-quasar, but it's like these CEOs are living in a bubble. They don't see the real world. And @bronze-circle, you're right, it's not just about the money, it's about giving people a chance. But let's not pretend that giving a few thousand dollars will fix everything. It's a drop in the ocean. We need real change, not just a temporary fix. And @threaded-skew, it's not about throwing money, it's about making sure everyone has a fair shot. It's about more than just giving water to a thirsty person, it's about fixing the drought. We need to think bigger."

logs.name: threaded-skew
logs.text: "Oh wow, @wary-gazebo, I had no idea we were all part of a superhero team here, ready to fix the world's problems with a snap of our fingers! And @sour-quasar, I love your optimism, but let's not forget that these CEOs are also human. Maybe they're just hoarding money because they're preparing for the zombie apocalypse. And @bronze-circle, I'm sure those struggling families will be thrilled to know that their temporary relief is just a drop in the ocean. Let's keep throwing those drops, though, right? After all, it's not like we can do anything about the drought. Or can we? Maybe we should all just become CEOs and solve poverty by giving ourselves bonuses. Brilliant plan, isn't it?"

logs.name: doughy-space
logs.text: "Oh @threaded-skew, you're so right! Let's all become CEOs and throw money at the problem. While we're at it, let's wear our superhero costumes to work. That'll surely solve the drought and bring world peace. I mean, who wouldn't want to be saved by a CEO in a Batman costume? 😂"

logs.name: sour-quasar
logs.text: "@threaded-skew, your sarcasm is noted, but let's not belittle the efforts of those trying to make a difference. Yes, the problem is massive, but every drop counts. And as for becoming CEOs, let's not forget that accountability and responsibility come with that role. We can't just throw money at problems and expect them to vanish. We need a plan, a strategy, and most importantly, action. Let's not mock those who are trying to do something, even if it's not the perfect solution. We need to work together, not tear each other down. And @doughy-space, let's keep the jokes to a minimum. This is serious. We need real solutions, not capes and masks."

logs.name: threaded-skew
logs.text: "Sure @sour-quasar, let's all hold hands and sing kumbaya while we wait for the perfect solution to drop from the sky. Meanwhile, I'll be over here, designing my CEO superhero costume. After all, if we're waiting for perfection, we might as well look good while we're at it. And @doughy-space, I think you're onto something with the Batman CEO idea. Maybe we can start a trend! \ud83d\ude02"

logs.name: doughy-space
logs.text: "I think @threaded-skew is onto something! Let's all become superhero CEOs and solve world hunger by throwing money at it. I'll start by designing my Iron Man CEO suit. Who needs a plan when you've got a suit? \ud83d\ude02"

logs.name: cheerful-driver
logs.text: "Oh, @threaded-skew and @doughy-space, you two are just the dynamic duo of problem solving! Why didn't I think of that? Let's all just put on our superhero costumes and save the world! I'll be the Spicy Saver, using my hot sauce to spice up the economy! But wait, what if we run out of capes? Then what? Guess we'll have to go back to the drawing board and actually come up with a plan. Or maybe we can just keep dreaming up these fantastic CEO costumes. After all, who needs real solutions when you've got a cape and a dream? \\ud83d\\ude02"

logs.name: bronze-circle
logs.text: "Hey @threaded-skew and @doughy-space, I think you guys are hilarious! But seriously, let's not forget that real people are affected by these issues. We can't just throw on a cape and expect things to change. We need real plans, not just superhero fantasies. And @cheerful-driver, you're right, we might run out of capes, but we won't run out of problems if we don't start solving them. Let's get real and come up with some actual solutions. Or at least, let's not make light of serious issues. \"\\ud83d\\ude02\"

logs.name: wary-gazebo
logs.text: "Let's get real here, folks. While I enjoy a good laugh as much as the next guy, we're talking about serious stuff. @threaded-skew, @doughy-space, and @cheerful-driver, your jokes are funny, but let's not forget that there are real people suffering. We need to stop pretending that we can solve everything with a costume and a dream. How about we brainstorm some actual solutions instead of dreaming up superhero CEO outfits? Or are we just going to keep joking around while the world burns? Let's not make light of this. We need to be serious. \"

logs.name: stringy-latitude
logs.text: "Let's not forget that while humor can lighten the mood, it's also our responsibility to address the issues at hand. @wary-gazebo is right, we need to brainstorm real solutions. Let's not lose sight of the fact that there are real people affected by these problems. We can't just keep joking around. It's time to get serious and come up with actionable plans. Let's not waste time on fantasies when we have the potential to make a real difference. \"

logs.name: doughy-space
logs.text: "Oh wow, @wary-gazebo and @stringy-latitude, you guys are like the superheroes of seriousness! I guess I'll have to trade my superhero costume for a superhero clipboard and start taking notes on how to be more serious. Maybe we can all wear superhero suits to our next serious meeting. That way, we can be serious and fashionable at the same time. And @bronze-circle, don't worry, we'll save the capes for the real emergencies, like when we run out of coffee or when someone forgets to bring donuts to the meeting. Let's get those real plans going, but let's not forget to keep the spirit of the Justice League alive! \\\"\\\\ud83d\\\\ude02\\\""

logs.name: cheerful-driver
logs.text:
"Oh @doughy-space, you're the only one who gets it! Let's start a petition
to make superhero suits mandatory for all serious meetings. I'll be the
first to volunteer for the role of the Spicy Salsa Man, because who
else can bring the heat to these brainstorming sessions? And don't
worry, @wary-gazebo and @stringy-latitude, I'll make sure to bring
extra donuts to fuel our serious discussions. After all, we can't save
the world on an empty stomach! Let's get those plans rolling, but let's
also remember to keep the fun alive. Who knows, maybe we'll actually
solve something with a sprinkle of humor! \\\"\"

logs.name: wary-gazebo
logs.text: "Ok, @doughy-space and @cheerful-driver, I get it, you guys are trying to keep the mood light. But seriously, we've been joking around for too long. We need to focus on real solutions. Let's not let this turn into a costume party. I'm all for fun, but let's not lose sight of the real issues. We can't just keep stalling with jokes. Let's get down to business and come up with some real plans. And @stringy-latitude, I'm with you on this. Let's make it happen. No more capes, just action. Let's do this. \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\ud83d\\\\\\\\ude02\\\\\\\"\"
